# AUTO-GENERATED by split_acceptance_tests.py
# Each original test function was split into one function per top-level
# assertion so pytest reports per-check pass/fail counts.
import pytest
from sqlglot.tokens import TokenType, Tokenizer

def test_identifier_escaped_quote_is_single_quote_in_token_text__a1():
    """
    When an identifier is quoted with ", and an embedded " is escaped as "",
    the tokenizer should produce a single " in the IDENTIFIER token text.

    This regressed when the implementation appended 2 * identifier_end (i.e. '""')
    instead of a single identifier_end ('"').
    """
    sql = 'SELECT "a""b"'
    tokens = Tokenizer().tokenize(sql)
    identifiers = [t for t in tokens if t.token_type == TokenType.IDENTIFIER]
    assert len(identifiers) == 1, f'Expected exactly one IDENTIFIER token for {sql!r}, got {len(identifiers)}: {tokens}'

def test_identifier_escaped_quote_is_single_quote_in_token_text__a2():
    """
    When an identifier is quoted with ", and an embedded " is escaped as "",
    the tokenizer should produce a single " in the IDENTIFIER token text.

    This regressed when the implementation appended 2 * identifier_end (i.e. '""')
    instead of a single identifier_end ('"').
    """
    sql = 'SELECT "a""b"'
    tokens = Tokenizer().tokenize(sql)
    identifiers = [t for t in tokens if t.token_type == TokenType.IDENTIFIER]
    assert identifiers[0].text == 'a"b', f'Expected escaped identifier quote "" inside "..." to be tokenized as a single quote in the identifier text. Got IDENTIFIER.text={identifiers[0].text!r} for SQL {sql!r}.'
